creative_brief: Hero image: a parent and child at a farm hub pickup, brown paper bags with visible fresh produce, handwritten farm name tags visible on packaging. Warm natural light, no supermarket aesthetic. Earthy palette — greens, kraft browns, warm whites. Photography style: documentary-real, not staged.
headline: I fed my family for years without asking a single honest question
subhead: Not where the meat came from. Not how far the eggs travelled. Not whether the farmer who grew our vegetables could actually make a living. FarmThru exists because I finally asked — and the answers changed everything.
hero_copy: Here's the confession. I thought choosing 'organic' at the supermarket meant I was doing the right thing. I wasn't. I was buying a label, not a relationship. I had no idea my groceries travelled an average of 2,400km through warehouses and wholesalers before reaching the shelf. I couldn't name one farm. Not one. When I discovered FarmThru — a farm-direct grocery store connecting families straight to regenerative farms across NSW — I realised the question was never 'what am I eating?' It was 'who am I feeding when I buy this?' Now I know every farm by name. And you can too.

sections.heading: The question that unravels everything
sections.body:
Ask your supermarket who grew your eggs. They can't tell you. Ask them how the pork farmer is paid. Silence. That silence isn't accidental — it's structural. The big chains built supply lines with warehouses, wholesalers, and middlemen between you and the people growing your food. Every link in that chain takes a cut. Farmers earn less. You pay more. And nobody can look you in the eye and say where it came from.

FarmThru strips all of that out. Zero warehouses. Zero wholesalers. Zero middlemen. Food moves from farm to hub to you. That's it. Bundarra Farm's pork belly. Farmer Brown's eggs. Collins Wild Caught's seasonal seafood straight from the South Coast. Paris Creek Farms' carbon-neutral biodynamic dairy from Meadows, SA. You don't just see a label — you see the farm, the farmer, the practice.

sections.heading: This isn't a subscription box — it's a grocery store with a conscience
sections.body:
FarmThru partners with around 15–20 farms and suppliers across NSW and beyond. You order online when you want. No membership fees. No lock-in contracts. No commitment. Buy in bulk and save, or just grab what you need this week.

Collect from the Brookvale hub, Monday to Friday. They also offer delivery across Sydney, the Central Coast, and Wollongong.

Rachel Ward — actor turned regenerative farming advocate, running Rachel's Farm in Kempsey, NSW — is a key partner and supplier. She didn't lend her name to a logo. She grows the food. That's the difference. Every supplier in the FarmThru network follows the same principle: regenerative practice, direct relationship, fair compensation.

sections.heading: Knowing isn't enough — be part of building it
sections.body:
FarmThru is preparing an equity crowdfunding campaign. That means everyday people — not venture capital, not the big chains — can own a piece of a food system that actually works for families and farmers.

This is early. There are real risks. The company is young, the model is growing, and like any investment, you could lose what you put in. But if you believe grocery shopping should fund regenerative farms instead of boardrooms, this is your chance to vote for that with your wallet.

Join the waitlist now. A $5 refundable deposit holds your spot. No obligation beyond that. When the campaign opens, you'll be first to see the details and decide for yourself.

sections.heading: One question changed how I feed my family
sections.body:
I stopped asking 'is this organic?' and started asking 'do I know who grew this?' That single shift led me to FarmThru. Now I pick up groceries from the Brookvale hub, and my daughter knows that her eggs come from Farmer Brown. She knows the salmon is wild-caught off the South Coast by Collins Wild Caught. She asks about the farms by name.

That's not a marketing story. That's Tuesday.

We'll show you where your food comes from. Join the waitlist and be part of what comes next.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru is preparing an equity crowdfunding campaign. This page is not a financial product offer. Any future offer will be made under a CSF offer document via a licensed intermediary. Equity crowdfunding involves risks, including the potential loss of your entire investment. Past performance is not indicative of future results. No returns are projected or guaranteed. You should consider whether this type of investment is appropriate for your circumstances and seek independent financial advice if needed.
